title: Systems and methods for vertebral adjustment
abstract: A system for non-invasively adjusting the curvature of a spine includes a housing having a first end and a second end, a first rod having a first end telescopically disposed within a cavity of the housing along a first longitudinal axis at the first end of the housing and having a first threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a first portion of a spinal system of a subject, a second rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity along a second longitudinal axis at the second end of the housing and having a second threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a second portion of the spinal system of the subject, a driving member rotatably disposed within the cavity and configured to be activated from a location external to the body of the subject.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for non-invasively adjusting the curvature of a spine comprising: a housing having a first end and a second end and a cavity between the first end and the second end; a first rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a first longitudinal axis at the first end of the housing and having a first threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a first portion of a spinal system of a subject; a second rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a second longitudinal axis at the second end of the housing and having a second threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a second portion of the spinal system of the subject; a driving member rotatably disposed within the cavity of the housing and configured to be activated from a location external to the body of the subject; a first interface rotationally coupling a first threaded driver to the driving member, the first threaded driver threadmgly engaging the first threaded portion of the first rod; a second interface rotationally coupling a second threaded driver to the driving member, the second threaded driver threadingly engaging the second threaded portion of the second rod; and wherein rotation of the driving member in a first direction causes the first threaded driver to move the first end of the first rod into the cavity of the housing along the first longitudinal axis and causes the second threaded driver to move the first end of the second rod into the cavity of the housing along the second longitudinal axis. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the first longitudinal axis is substantially co- linear with the second longitudinal axis. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the driving member rotates about a third longitudinal axis and wherein no two of the first, second, and third longitudinal axes are co- linear. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the first and third longitudinal axes define a first angle and the second and third longitudinal axes define a second angle. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the first angle is substantially equal to the second angle. 6. The system of claim 4, wherein the first angle is different from the second angle. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein rotation of the driving member in a second direction, opposite the first direction, causes the first threaded driver to move the first end of the first rod out of the cavity of the housing along the first longitudinal axis and causes the second threaded driver to move the first end of the second rod out of the cavity of the housing along the second longitudinal axis. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the first driving member and second driving member comprise a turnbuckle. 9. The system of claim 1 wherein the first threaded portion of the first rod and the first threaded driver comprise right-handed threads and the second threaded portion of the second rod and the second threaded driver comprise left-handed threads. 10. The system of claim 1 wherein the first threaded portion of the first rod and the first threaded driver comprise left-handed threads and the second threaded portion of the second rod and the second threaded driver comprise right-handed threads. 1 1. The system of claim 1 , wherein at least one of the first threaded driver and second threaded driver comprises a screw. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the other of the first threaded driver and second threaded driver comprises a screw. 13. The system of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first threaded driver and second threaded driver comprises a female thread. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the other of the first threaded driver and second threaded driver comprises a female thread. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein at least one of the female thread of the first threaded driver and second threaded driver comprises a nut. 16. The system of claim 1, wherein the first threaded portion of the first rod comprises a female thread. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the second threaded portion of the second rod comprises a female thread, 18. The system of claim 1, wherein the first threaded portion of the first rod comprises a male thread. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the second threaded portion of the second rod comprises a male thread. 20. The system of claim 1, wherein the first rod and second rod each have diameters of about 3.5 mm or greater. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the first rod and second rod each have diameters of about 4.5 mm or greater. 22. The system of claim 21, wherein the first rod and second rod each have diameters of about 5.5 mm or greater. 23. The system of claim 1, wherein rotation of the driving member in the first direction causes an increase in a traction force between the housing and the first portion of the spinal system. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein rotation of the driving member in the first direction causes an increase in a traction force between the housing and the second portion of the spinal system. 25. The system of claim 7, wherein rotation of the driving member in the second direction causes a decrease in a traction force between the housing and the first portion of the spinal system. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein rotation of the driving member in the second direction causes a decrease in a traction force between the housing and the second portion of the spinal system. 27. The system of claim 1 , wherein the first portion of the spinal system comprises a first vertebra. 28. The system of claim 27, wherein the first vertebra comprises a lumbar vertebra. 29. The system of either of claims 27 or 28, wherein the second portion of the spinal system comprises a second vertebra, different from the first vertebra. 30. The system of claim 29, wherein the second vertebra comprises a lumbar vertebra, 31. The system of claim 1, wherein the second end of the first rod is configured to be coupled to a vertebra with a pedicle screw. 32. The system of claim 31, wherein the second end of the second rod is configured to be coupled to a vertebra with a pedicle screw. 33. The system of claim 1, wherein the housing is configured to be coupled to a third portion of the spinal system. 34. The system of either of claims 29 or 30, wherein the housing is configured to be coupled to a third vertebra, different from the first vertebra and second vertebra. 35. The system of claim 34, wherein rotation of the driving member in the first direction causes an increase in a traction force between the third vertebra and at least one of the first and second vertebrae. 36. The system of claim 35, wherein rotation of the driving member in a second direction causes a decrease in a traction force between the third vertebra and the at least one of the first and second vertebrae. 37. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a first slip clutch disposed between the driving member and first threaded driver. 38. The system of claim 1, further comprising a second slip clutch disposed between the driving member and the second threaded driver. 39. The system of claim 1, further comprising a slip clutch disposed between the first threaded driver and the second threaded driver. 40. The system of claim 1, further comprising a differential disposed between the driving member and first threaded driver. 41. The system of claim 1, further comprising a differential disposed between the driving member and the second threaded driver. 42. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a differential disposed between the first threaded driver and the second threaded driver. 43. The system of any of claims 40 to 42, wherein the differential comprises differential gears. 44. The system of claim 43, wherein the differential gears comprise planetary gears. 45. The system of any of claims 40 to 42, wherein rotation of the driving member in the first direction causes an increase in a first traction force between the housing and the first portion of the spinal system and an increase in a second traction force between the housing and the second portion of the spinal system. 46. The system of claim 45, wherein the differential is configured such that the driving member continues to rotating in the first direction and increase the first traction force when the first traction force is below a first threshold force and the second traction force is above a second traction force. 47. The system of claim 1 , wherein the first rod, second rod, and the housing have anti-rotation features configured to limit rotation between the first rod and the housing and the second rod and the housing. 48. The system of claim 1, further comprising a first rotational bearing coupled to the driving member. 49. The system of claim 48, wherein the rotational bearing comprises a radial bearing. 50. The system of claim 48, wherein the rotational bearing comprises a thrust bearing. 51. The system of claim 48, further comprising a second rotational bearing coupled to the driving member. 52. The system of claim 51, wherein the first rotational bearing and the second rotational bearing are disposed at opposite ends of the driving member. 53. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a dynamic seal between the first rod and the housing. 54. The system of claim 53, wherein the dynamic seal is disposed between an exterior portion of the first rod and an interior portion of the housing. 55. The system of claim 53, wherein the dynamic seal comprises at least one o- ring. 56. The system of claim 1 , wherein at least one of the second end of the first rod and second end of the second rod is coupled to the spinal system by a pivotable interface. 57. The system of claim 56, wherein the pivotable interface comprises a pivot interposed between the first rod and a pedicle screw, the pedicle screw coupled to a vertebra of the subject. 58. The system of claim 57, wherein the first rod comprises a main rod portion and a transverse extension rod portion, the transverse extension rod portion passing through the pedicle screw. 59. The system of any of claims 56 to 58, wherein the pivotable interface can be locked and unlocked. 60. The system of claim 59, wherein the pivotable interface can be selectively- locked and unlocked by a user. 61. The system of claim 59, wherein the pivotable interface can be non-invasively locked and unlocked by a user. 62. The system of claim 56, wherein the second portion of the first rod is configured to be coupled to a dorsal portion of a first vertebra and the second portion of the second rod is configured to be coupled to a dorsal portion of a second vertebra, different from the first vertebra, and wherein movement of the first end of the first rod into the cavity of the housing and movement of the first end of the second rod into the cavity of the housing cause the dorsal portion of the first vertebra and the dorsal portion of the second vertebra to move toward one another. 63. The system of claim 62, wherein the dorsal portion of the first vertebra and the dorsal portion of the second vertebra as caused to move toward one another at a faster rate than an anterior portion of the first vertebra is caused to move toward an anterior portion of the second vertebra, thus causing an increase in lordosis between the first vertebra and the second vertebra, as defined by a sagittal plane. 64. The system of claim 59, wherein rotation of the driving member causes the pivotable interface to be intermittently locked and unlocked. 65. The system of claim 63, wherein rotation of the driving member causes the pivotable interface to be intermittently placed in a locked condition and an unlocked condition, such that the increase in lordosis may be imparted between the first vertebra and the second vertebra when the pivotable interface is the unlocked condition, but not when the pivotable interface is in the locked condition. 66. The system of claim 64, further comprising a cam coupled to the driving member, the cam configured to unlock the pivotable interface. 67. The system of claim 64, further comprising a cam coupled to the driving member, the cam configured to lock the pivotable interface. 68. The system of claim 64, further comprising a cam coupled to the driving member, the cam configured to lock and unlock the pivotable interface. 69. The system of claim 1, wherein the driving member comprises a Neodymium- Iron-Boron magnet. 70. The system of claim 1, wherein the driving member is defined by or is coupled to a driving element which is selected from the group consisting of: a permanent magnet, and inductively coupled motor, an ultrasonically actuated motor, a subcutaneous hydraulic pump, a shape memory-driven actuator, and a magnetorestrictive element. 71. The system of claim 70, wherein the driving element comprises a radially- poled permanent magnet configured for rotation within the housing. 72. The system of claim 71, wherein the radially-poled permanent magnet is configured to be rotated by application of an externally applied rotating magnetic field. 73. The system of claim 72, further comprising: an external remote controller configured to produce a rotating magnetic field capable of rotating the radially-poled permanent magnet. 74. The system of claim 73, wherein the external remote controller comprises at least one electromagnet. 75. The system of claim 73, wherein the external remote controller comprises at least one rotatable permanent magnet. 76. The system of claim 75, wherein the external remote controller comprises at least two rotatable permanent magnets. 77. The system of claim 62, wherein the second portion of the first rod is configured to be coupled to the first vertebra with a pedicle screw and the second portion of the second rod is configured to be coupled to the second vertebra with a pedicle screw. 78. The system of claim 62, wherein the housing is configured to be coupled to a third vertebra, different from the first vertebra or second vertebra. 79. The system of claim 78, wherein the third vertebra is between the first vertebra and the second vertebra. 80. The system of claim 78, wherein the housing is configured to be coupled to the third vertebra with a pedicle screw. 81. A method for adjusting the curvature of a spine comprising: providing a non-invasively adjustable system comprising: a housing having a first end and a second end and a cavity extending between the first end and the second end; a first rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a first longitudinal axis at the first end of the housing and having a first threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a first portion of a spinal system of a subject; a second rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a second longitudinal axis at the second end of the housing and having a second threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a second portion of the spinal system of the subject; a driving member rotatably disposed within the cavity of the housing and configured to be activated from a location external to the body of the subject; a first interface rotationally coupling a first threaded driver to the driving member, the first threaded driver threadingly engaging the first threaded portion of the first rod; and a second interface rotationally coupling a second threaded driver to the driving member, the second threaded driver threadingly engaging the second threaded portion of the second rod, wherein rotation of the driving member in a first direction causes the first threaded driver to move the first end of the first rod into the cavity of the housing along the first longitudinal axis and causes the second threaded driver to move the first end of the second rod into the cavity of the housing along the second longitudinal axis; creating an opening in the skin of a patient as part of a lumbar fusion surgery; 

 coupling the second end of the first rod to a dorsal portion of a first vertebra of the patient; coupling the second end of the second rod to a dorsal portion of a second v ertebra of the patient; and closing or causing to close the opening in the skin of the patient. 82. The method of claim 81, further comprising: identifying an angle of curvature in the sagittal plane of a lumbar region at a time after the lumbar fusion surgery. 83. The method of claim. 82, further comprising: non-invasively causing rotation in the driving member to change the angle of curvature in the sagittal plane of the lumbar region. 84. The method of claim 84, wherein the step of non-invasively causing rotation includes causing the first threaded driver to move the first end of the first rod into the cavity of the housing along the first longitudinal axis and causing the second threaded driver to move the first end of the second rod into the cavity of the housing along the second longitudinal axis. 89. The system of claim 37, wherein the first slip clutch is configured to couple rotational motion between the driving member and the first threaded driver when the driving member turns in the first direction and to decouple rotational motion between the driving member and the first threaded driver when the driving member turns in a second direction, opposite the first direction. 90. A method for adjusting the curvature of a spine comprising: providing a non-invasively adjustable system comprising: a housing having a first end and a second end and a cavity extending between the first end and the second end; a first rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a first longitudinal axis at the first end of the housing and having a first threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a first portion of a spinal system of a subject; a second rod having a first end telescopically disposed within the cavity of the housing along a second longitudinal axis at the second end of the housing and having a second threaded portion extending thereon, and a second end configured to be coupled to a second portion of the spinal system of the subject; a driving member rotatably disposed within the cavity of the housing and configured to be activated from a location external to the body of the subject; a first interface rotationally coupling a first threaded driver to the driving member, the first threaded driver threadingly engaging the first threaded portion of the first rod; and
